findings.message: ItemCreate schema accepts owner_email but post_item endpoint discards it: create_item() is called with only payload.name, not payload.owner_email. This creates a silent data loss pattern where clients submit PII that is never persisted.
findings.suggestion: Either remove owner_email from ItemCreate schema if it should not be accepted, or update create_item() signature to accept and persist owner_email: change create_item(session, payload.name) to create_item(session, payload.name, payload.owner_email)

raw_text: [{"path":"src/demo/routes/items.py","line":44,"severity":"medium","message":"ItemCreate schema accepts owner_email but post_item endpoint discards it: create_item() is called with only payload.name, not payload.owner_email. This creates a silent data loss pattern where clients submit PII that is never persisted.","suggestion":"Either remove owner_email from ItemCreate schema if it should not be accepted, or update create_item() signature to accept and persist owner_email: change create_item(session, payload.name) to create_item(session, payload.name, payload.owner_email)"}]
